monday.question: What does the word 'similar' mean?
What is the comparative form of the adjective 'big'?
This car is _____ (fast) than that one.
What does the word 'different' mean?
Choose the correct comparative: 'Tokyo is _____ than my hometown.'
She is _____ (tall) than her brother.
Brazil is _____ than Portugal in terms of size.
Translate to English: 'O Rio de Janeiro é mais quente do que São Paulo.'
monday.correctAnswer: rio de janeiro is hotter than são paulo|rio de janeiro is hotter than sao paulo

monday.question: What does the word 'comparison' mean?
options: Examining similarities and differences between things
A type of adjective used in English
A way of speaking more formally
monday.correctAnswer: Examining similarities and differences between things

monday.question: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: This phone is more expensive than the old one.
This phone is expensiver than the old one.
This phone is most expensive than the old one.
This phone is expensive than the old one.
monday.correctAnswer: This phone is more expensive than the old one.

monday.question: The bus is _____ (cheap) than the taxi.
This exercise is _____ than the last one. (use the comparative of 'difficult')
The word 'faster' is the comparative form of which adjective?
Translate to English: 'Este livro é menos interessante do que o outro.'
monday.correctAnswer: this book is less interesting than the other one|this book is less interesting than the other

monday.question: What is the comparative form of 'good'?
The new laptop is _____ expensive than the old one.
He speaks English _____ than I do. (comparative of 'well')
Translate to English: 'A minha cidade é menor do que a capital.'
monday.correctAnswer: my city is smaller than the capital

monday.question: Which sentence uses the comparative correctly?
options: He runs more fast than me.

monday.question: This hotel is _____ comfortable than the hostel. (use 'more' or 'less')

tuesday.question: What does the superlative phrase 'the best' express?
options: The highest quality among all options
Better than just one other thing
tuesday.correctAnswer: The highest quality among all options

tuesday.question: What is the superlative form of 'tall'?
Mount Everest is _____ (high) mountain in the world.
What does 'the worst' mean?
options: The lowest quality among all options
Worse than just one other thing
tuesday.correctAnswer: The lowest quality among all options

tuesday.question: Choose the correct superlative: 'This is _____ movie I've ever seen.'
She is _____ (smart) student in the entire class.
This is _____ (expensive) restaurant in town. I can't afford it!
Translate to English: 'Este é o restaurante mais caro da cidade.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: this is the most expensive restaurant in the city

tuesday.question: The phrase 'the most' is typically used to form superlatives of adjectives that are...
Which is the correct superlative of 'bad'?
That was _____ (good) meal I have ever had! Absolutely delicious.
This hostel is _____ (cheap) accommodation option in the city.
What does the phrase 'the least' mean in a superlative comparison?
options: The minimum degree among all options
Less than one other thing only
tuesday.correctAnswer: The minimum degree among all options

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Ela é a aluna mais dedicada da turma.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: she is the most dedicated student in the class

tuesday.question: What is the superlative form of 'interesting'?
He is _____ (popular) singer in the country right now.
Physics is _____ (difficult) subject I have this semester.
Translate to English: 'Qual é o menor país do mundo?'
tuesday.correctAnswer: what is the smallest country in the world?

tuesday.question: Which sentence uses the superlative correctly?
options: She is more tallest in her family.
She is the tallest in her family.
She is tallest than her family.
She is most tall in her family.
tuesday.correctAnswer: She is the tallest in her family.

tuesday.question: This is _____ (least + comfortable) seat on the entire plane.

wednesday.question: What does the construction 'as...as' express in English?
options: Two things are equal in some quality
One thing is better than another
One thing is the best of all
wednesday.correctAnswer: Two things are equal in some quality

wednesday.question: Choose the correct sentence using 'as...as': 'She is _____ her mother.'
This laptop is not _____ expensive _____ that one. (as...as)
What does the adjective 'comfortable' mean?
options: Giving a feeling of physical ease and relaxation
wednesday.correctAnswer: Giving a feeling of physical ease and relaxation

wednesday.question: What does 'He is not as fast as his brother' mean?
options: His brother is faster than him
He is faster than his brother
They run at the same speed
He is the fastest in the family
wednesday.correctAnswer: His brother is faster than him

wednesday.question: He is _____ intelligent _____ his sister. (they have equal intelligence)
The bus is not _____ quick _____ the train. (as...as structure)
Translate to English: 'Este celular é tão rápido quanto o meu.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: this phone is as fast as mine|this cell phone is as fast as mine

wednesday.question: The adjective 'cheap' means...
Which sentence is correct?
options: This hotel is as cheap than mine.
This hotel is as cheap as mine.
This hotel is as cheaper as mine.
This hotel is as cheap that mine.
wednesday.correctAnswer: This hotel is as cheap as mine.

wednesday.question: Your house is _____ big _____ mine. (they are the same size)
She doesn't study _____ hard _____ she used to. (not as...as)
When two things are described as 'similar', it means they are...
options: Nearly equal or alike in some way
One is better than the other
wednesday.correctAnswer: Nearly equal or alike in some way

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'O metrô não é tão confortável quanto o ônibus expresso.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: the metro is not as comfortable as the express bus|the subway is not as comfortable as the express bus

wednesday.question: 'Not as fast as' is equivalent to which of the following?
In my opinion, Paris is _____ beautiful _____ Rome. (they are equally beautiful)
He isn't _____ experienced _____ his colleague at the company.
Translate to English: 'Ela corre tão rápido quanto ele.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: she runs as fast as him|she runs as fast as he does

wednesday.question: Rewrite using 'as...as': 'The red car is faster than the blue car.' Which option is closest in meaning?
options: The blue car is not as fast as the red car.
The red car is not as fast as the blue car.
Both cars are as fast as each other.
The blue car is faster than the red car.
wednesday.correctAnswer: The blue car is not as fast as the red car.

wednesday.question: This coffee is not _____ hot _____ the one I ordered. (as...as)

thursday.question: The connector 'whereas' is used to...
options: Show a direct contrast between two facts
thursday.correctAnswer: Show a direct contrast between two facts

thursday.question: Choose the correct use of 'whereas': '_____'
options: I love summer, whereas my sister prefers winter.
I love summer, whereas I also like spring.
I love summer, whereas it is hot.
I love summer, whereas summer is nice.
thursday.correctAnswer: I love summer, whereas my sister prefers winter.

thursday.question: _____ it was raining heavily, we still went for a walk. (concession connector)
thursday.correctAnswer: although|even though|though

thursday.question: The word 'unlike' is used to...
options: Highlight a difference between two people or things
thursday.correctAnswer: Highlight a difference between two people or things

thursday.question: Where is 'however' typically placed in a sentence?
options: At the start of a new sentence or after a semicolon
Always in the middle of a clause
Only at the end of a sentence
thursday.correctAnswer: At the start of a new sentence or after a semicolon

thursday.question: I love Italian food; _____, my sister cannot eat cheese. (contrast connector)
thursday.correctAnswer: however

thursday.question: _____ my brother, I don't enjoy watching sports on TV.
thursday.correctAnswer: unlike

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Embora seja caro, este restaurante é excelente.'
thursday.correctAnswer: although it is expensive, this restaurant is excellent|even though it is expensive, this restaurant is excellent

thursday.question: The word 'although' has a similar meaning to which of the following?
thursday.correctAnswer: even though / though

thursday.question: Which sentence uses 'although' correctly?
options: Although she studied hard, she passed the test.
She studied hard although she passed.
Although she passed, she studied hard.
She although studied hard passed the test.
thursday.correctAnswer: Although she studied hard, she passed the test.

thursday.question: She works extremely hard; _____, she never seems to get promoted.
thursday.correctAnswer: however

thursday.question: I love city life, _____ my best friend prefers the countryside.
thursday.correctAnswer: whereas|while

thursday.question: The connector 'however' is used to...
options: Introduce a contrasting or surprising idea
thursday.correctAnswer: Introduce a contrasting or surprising idea

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Ao contrário de mim, ela adora acordar cedo.'
thursday.correctAnswer: unlike me, she loves waking up early|unlike me, she loves to wake up early

thursday.question: Choose the best connector: 'The weather was terrible. _____, we had a fantastic holiday.'
thursday.correctAnswer: However

thursday.question: _____ cats are generally independent, dogs tend to be very loyal and social.
thursday.correctAnswer: whereas|while|although

thursday.question: _____ he studied all night long, he still failed the final exam.
thursday.correctAnswer: although|even though|though

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'No entanto, o projeto foi um grande sucesso.'
thursday.correctAnswer: however, the project was a great success

thursday.question: Which sentence contains an error in connector usage?
options: Although he was tired, he finished the work.
He was tired. However, he finished the work.
He was tired, whereas he finished the work.
Unlike his teammates, he finished the work.
thursday.correctAnswer: He was tired, whereas he finished the work.

thursday.question: _____ my initial expectations, the trip turned out to be absolutely amazing.
thursday.correctAnswer: unlike|contrary to

friday.question: What does the word 'contrast' mean?
options: A clear difference between two things
friday.correctAnswer: A clear difference between two things

friday.question: Which sentence uses the comparative correctly?
options: São Paulo is more populated than Rio de Janeiro.
São Paulo is more populateder than Rio de Janeiro.
São Paulo is populateder than Rio de Janeiro.
São Paulo is the more populated than Rio de Janeiro.
friday.correctAnswer: São Paulo is more populated than Rio de Janeiro.

friday.question: The Amazon River is one of _____ (long) rivers in the world.
friday.correctAnswer: the longest

friday.question: Which sentence best illustrates the use of 'whereas'?
options: He likes tea, whereas she prefers coffee.
He likes tea, whereas he also drinks coffee.
He likes tea, whereas tea is delicious.
He likes tea. Whereas, it is healthy.
friday.correctAnswer: He likes tea, whereas she prefers coffee.

friday.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'as...as'?
options: This test is as difficult as I expected.
This test is as difficult than I expected.
This test is as more difficult as I expected.
This test is as difficult that I expected.
friday.correctAnswer: This test is as difficult as I expected.

friday.question: A train ticket is _____ (expensive) than a bus ticket, but it's much faster.
friday.correctAnswer: more expensive

friday.question: In my opinion, Italy has _____ (delicious) food in all of Europe.
friday.correctAnswer: the most delicious

friday.question: Translate to English: 'O inverno no Canadá é muito mais frio do que no Brasil.'
friday.correctAnswer: winter in canada is much colder than in brazil|winter in canada is much colder than brazil

friday.question: The word 'unlike' introduces...
options: A contrast between two different things or people
The best example in a group
friday.correctAnswer: A contrast between two different things or people

friday.question: Choose the correct contrast connector: 'I passed all my exams. _____, my grades were not perfect.'
friday.correctAnswer: However

friday.question: My new apartment is not _____ spacious _____ my old one.
friday.correctAnswer: as spacious as|as / as

friday.question: My exam score was _____ (bad) this semester than last semester.
friday.correctAnswer: worse

friday.question: 'More expensive' is the comparative form of which adjective?
friday.correctAnswer: expensive

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Embora eu prefira o verão, o outono também é muito bonito.'
friday.correctAnswer: although i prefer summer, autumn is also very beautiful|even though i prefer summer, autumn is also very beautiful

friday.question: Which sentence is the most complete and correct comparison?
options: Although taxis are faster, the subway is cheaper and less stressful.
Although taxis are most fast, the subway is more cheap and less stressful.
Although taxis are faster, the subway is more cheap and least stressful.
Although taxis are faster, the subway is cheap and less stressful than.
friday.correctAnswer: Although taxis are faster, the subway is cheaper and less stressful.

friday.question: _____ English, Mandarin Chinese has a much more complex writing system.
friday.correctAnswer: unlike

friday.question: The new phone model is _____ powerful _____ the previous one. (as...as — equal power)
friday.correctAnswer: as powerful as

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Ao contrário do meu irmão, eu prefiro estudar de manhã.'
friday.correctAnswer: unlike my brother, i prefer to study in the morning|unlike my brother, i prefer studying in the morning

friday.question: Identify the sentence that correctly uses ALL structures from this lesson:
options: Although Paris is the most romantic city, it is not as cheap as Lisbon, whereas Lisbon is smaller.
Although Paris is most romantic city, it is not as cheap than Lisbon, whereas Lisbon is more small.
Although Paris is the more romantic city, it is not as cheap as Lisbon, whereas Lisbon is smaller.
Although Paris is the most romantic city, it is not as cheap than Lisbon, whereas Lisbon is the small.
friday.correctAnswer: Although Paris is the most romantic city, it is not as cheap as Lisbon, whereas Lisbon is smaller.

friday.question: This was _____ (interesting) course I have ever taken. I learned so much!
friday.correctAnswer: the most interesting
